# Checkpoint instance — `homelab`

*Generated by checkpoint/BOOTSTRAP — 2026-06-21; regenerate when gate/docs/conventions move.*

NixOS + home-manager flake managing 4 NixOS hosts (pi · aurora · workstation · pavilion)
+ a standalone home-manager macbook. **Infrastructure-as-config**, not application code:
the "build" is a NixOS system closure, the "tests" are eval/nixosTest/runtime-introspection
layers, and "deploy" mutates a real machine. The repo already runs the skill's exact mental
model under a different name — its `docs/invariants.md` **enforcement ladder**
(`prose → comment → runtime-introspection → test → type/lint/CI`) *is* the derivation ladder.
Lean on it; don't reinvent it.

---

## §1 — The gate

| Grade | Command | Real-exit form | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **G0** (per-file) | `just check FILE` — *does not exist here*; use `nix fmt -- <file>` + `statix check <file>` | direct exit | No single-file Lean-style check. Closest fast loop is `just test-eval` (sub-second, all `eval-*` checks). |
| **G1** (per-commit) | **`nix flake check`** (alias `just check`) | direct exit code; **never pipe** (`… | head` masks failure — see MEMORY `nix-build-verify-exit-codes`) | The canonical "is it green." Runs statix + deadnix + nixfmt + lint(TOML registry) + all custom guard derivations + eval tests + `docs-fresh`. |
| **G2** (feature/wrap) | `nix flake check` **+ `just test`** (runtime-introspection composite, ~3 min) + blast-radius survey | each recipe `exit 1` on fail; composite is one signal | `just test` requires a **live deployed fleet** (SSHes pi/aurora, curls VictoriaMetrics). Only meaningful post-deploy on a real host. |
| **G3** (release/deploy) | `just rebuild` (this host) / `just rebuild-homelab` (fan-out) / `just deploy` (from origin) | **MUTATES A REAL SYSTEM — never run from an agent unprompted** | The push gate (`just show-pending-diff` + operator OK) is the formal boundary; `git push origin/main` is gated, local commits are not. |

**Guards that run around the gate** (all inside `nix flake check`):
- `statix` (anti-patterns) · `deadnix` (unused bindings, `--no-lambda-pattern-names`) · `format` (`nixfmt-tree --ci`)
- `lint` — TOML rule registry (`lint/rules.toml`), one grep-shaped derivation, ~10 rules
- Standalone guard derivations: `every-service-has-fs-hardening` · `every-service-has-backup-intent` · `infra-concerns-have-tests` · `routing-coherence` · `docs-fresh`
- Eval tests: `eval-lanroute-customdns` · `eval-lanroute-port-validation` · `eval-route-invariants` · `eval-gatus-probes`
- nixosTests (layer 2): `e2e-pi-smoke` · `e2e-multi-host` · `e2e-restic-backup` · `e2e-disk-alert` (slow; in `tests/`)
- **Pre-commit hook** `.githooks/pre-commit` runs `nix flake check` on staged `.nix`/`flake.lock`. **Skips silently when `nix` is off PATH** (the Mac dev box — the common case). CI is the backstop.
- **CI** `.github/workflows/check.yml` = `nix flake check --print-build-logs` on push+PR.

**Baseline (known-red the gate tolerates):** none structural. `just check-migration`
(`path-coherence`, `multi-line-comments`) is **demoted off the gate** — run on demand
during restructures only (near-nil steady-state catch rate). Treat its findings as
advisory, not gate failures.

**Last-green marker:** no git tags. The checkpoint ledger is plain `git diff <last-green>..`;
`origin/main` is the deploy boundary, so `origin/main..HEAD` is the natural "since last shipped" window.

---

## §2 — Artifact-volatility map

`docs/` is tier-encoded by location; `CLAUDE.md` + `docs/README.md` carry the routing tables.

| Artifact | Diátaxis mode | Rung | In blast radius when… |
|---|---|---|---|
| **`docs/generated/{lan-route,topology,capabilities,backups,fs,replicas}.md`** | Reference | **GENERATE** (`nix build .#docs-<X>`, drift-gated by `docs-fresh`) | a touched schema regen — **autofix**, never hand-edit. `docs-fresh` fails the gate on drift; regen cmd is printed by the check. |
| **`docs/invariants.md`** | Reference (the catalog) | hand-maintained, but each *claim* is rung-tagged | any structural change, any new rule, any promotion/demotion of a check. The single home for load-bearing claims. |
| `docs/reference/*.md` (topology, storage, network, services, module-authoring, testing-methodology, runtime-tests, recovery, capacity-baseline, agentic-workflow, documentation-writing) | Reference (HIGH volatility) | survey (`routing-coherence` gates *existence+routing*, not content) | the WHAT they describe moves — schema/option/host/pattern changes. Pair with the generated doc that carries the field-level WHAT. |
| `docs/glossary.md` | Reference | survey | vocabulary changes (new `nori.<X>` family, new tier/audience value). |
| `docs/roadmap.md`, `docs/plans/*.md` | volatile-state / How-to | survey — **always in blast radius for in-flight work** | a plan's phase lands or is rejected (e.g. `2026-06-21-dendritic-evaluation.md` marked PARTIALLY IMPLEMENTED). |
| `docs/decisions/*.md` (ADRs) | Explanation | survey (+ status lifecycle) | a hard-to-reverse choice is made/changed. **Append-only**: supersede (`Status: Superseded by ADR-XXXX`), never delete — preserves the rejected-alternative trail. README is the format SoT. |
| `docs/installs/*`, `docs/runbooks/*`, `docs/specs/*`, `docs/reports/*` | Tutorial / How-to / Explanation (LOW) | survey, rarely | bring-up/recovery procedure or a spec'd design changes. |
| `.claude/skills/gotcha-*/SKILL.md` (40+) | How-to (landmine procedures) | survey | a new landmine is hit, or a documented one is structurally fixed (then the gotcha can retire). |
| `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/README.md`, `secrets/README.md`, `README.md` | Reference (agent-facing) | survey (`routing-coherence` gates the routing tables resolve) | host/module/convention map changes; doc kind added/moved. |
| `flake.lock` | (lockfile, not a doc) | partial-test (eval pins it; no drift-error gate) | input re-pin (`just update-flake`) — deliberate, see §4b deps climb. |

**Generated (leaves the survey):** the six `docs/generated/*.md`. **Volatile-state (always in radius):** `docs/roadmap.md` + the active `docs/plans/*.md`.

---

## §3 — Work-type → survey table

Commit convention: **Conventional Commits** (`feat(scope):`, `fix(scope):`, `refactor(scope):`, `docs(scope):`, `chore(scope):`), scope = the concern (`flake`, `networking`, `testing`, `claude`, `plans`, …). Work is shaped **per-PR** (one feature/improvement), three phases Prologue → Execution → Epilogue (`docs/reference/agentic-workflow.md`, ADR-0005).

| Work-type | Tends to invalidate → survey shortlist |
|---|---|
| `feat(<infra-concern>)` (new/changed `nori.<X>` schema) | the matching **`docs/generated/<X>.md`** (autofix via `docs-fresh`) · its `docs/reference/*.md` WHY-pair · `docs/invariants.md` (new claim? new rung?) · **does it ship a `just test-<X>`?** (`infra-concerns-have-tests` will fail the gate if not) · `docs/glossary.md` if vocabulary moved. |
| `feat(services)` (new workload) | `every-service-has-fs-hardening` + `every-service-has-backup-intent` **will block** if missing — gate catches these. Survey: `docs/reference/services.md` (backup pattern A/B/C), topology placement (workhorse-by-default), `docs/reference/capacity-baseline.md`. |
| `feat(networking)` (`nori.lanRoutes` entry) | `eval-route-invariants` + `routing-coherence` gate-check. Survey: `docs/generated/lan-route.md` (autofix), `docs/reference/network.md`, audience↔auth coupling (`audience=family` ⇒ oidc/forwardAuth/noAuthReason assertion). **Multi-host:** `just rebuild-homelab`, not just local. |
| `fix(<scope>)` | usually narrow. A gotcha incident → add/update `.claude/skills/gotcha-*/`. No doc survey unless it changes a documented WHAT. |
| `refactor(flake)` / restructure | `routing-coherence` + `path-coherence` (run `just check-migration`). Survey: every doc referencing moved module paths; `docs/plans/<the-restructure>.md` phase status. |
| `chore`, pure rename, comment typo | **G0/G1 only — skip the survey** (anti-trigger). |
| `decision` (ADR) | new `docs/decisions/NNNN-*.md` (status lifecycle); supersede links bidirectional; `docs/invariants.md` if it changes an enforced claim. |
| Any change to a **check/rule** | `docs/invariants.md` (the ladder catalog + promotion work-list) — the SoT for what's enforced and at which rung. |

---

## §4 — Idiosyncrasies (the non-obvious "what must stay true")

From CLAUDE.md "Hard rules" + `docs/invariants.md` catalog:

1. **Code is the single source of truth; docs approximate.** `[judgment]` — the framing under everything.
2. **`disko*.nix` references disks by `/dev/disk/by-id/*`, never `/dev/nvmeN`** — NVMe enumeration is unstable across reboots. `[law: lint.diskoUsesById]`. *Never touch `nvme0n1` without verifying the model via by-id.*
3. **Don't commit secrets.** `secrets/secrets.yaml` is sops-encrypted (safe committed). `.env` gitignored. Public certs fine. **Never bulk-rename keys in sops yaml** (AAD-bound ciphertext breaks) — `[prose: unchecked]`, gotcha skill. **Never `sops -d` to inspect** (MEMORY: agent reading plaintext *is* the leak).
4. **Don't bypass the safety net.** No disabling `restic.backups.*`, `btrbk.*`, `OnFailure → ntfy` without naming why + re-enable plan.
5. **Default-deny everywhere.** Network/FS/tailnet ports opt-in, never wildcard. Firewall `[structural]`.
6. **Every service module declares `nori.harden.<unit>`** (or named exclusion) — `[law: every-service-has-fs-hardening]`.
7. **Every service has backup intent** (`nori.backups.<svc>.include` XOR `.skip = <reason>`) — `[law: every-service-has-backup-intent]` + `[runtime: just test-backups]` (fresh snapshot ≤25h/target).
8. **Every Reader-shaped `modules/infra/<X>/` ships a `just test-<X>`** — `[law: infra-concerns-have-tests]`. Adding an infra concern without its layer-3 test fails the gate.
9. **Tailnet IS the auth perimeter; Authelia only for per-user identity.** Encoded as the typed `audience` enum (`operator`/`family`/`public`) — `[structural]` + assertion (`family` ⇒ oidc/forwardAuth/noAuthReason).
10. **One `nori.lanRoutes` entry → Caddy + Blocky DNS + Gatus + (opt) Authelia OIDC**, consistently. `[structural]` single-schema-multi-generator + `[runtime: just test-routes]`.
11. **Function-named subdomains** (`status` not `gatus`) unless brand IS identity — `[law: lint.functionNamedSubdomains]`.
12. **`nori.<X>` effects are one input → multiple generators** (Reader + collected-Writer shape). The composable-abstractions-not-god-modules bias.
13. **Committed generated docs must match generator output** — `[law: docs-fresh]`. Regenerate + commit; never hand-edit `docs/generated/*`.
14. **Push gate:** agents NEVER `git push origin/main` without `git log -p origin/main..HEAD` / `just show-pending-diff` surfaced inline + explicit operator OK. Local commits ungated.
15. **`just rebuild` mutates the live machine you're sitting on.** Multi-host changes need `just rebuild-homelab` (fan-out) to avoid split-brain. From the Mac, recipes run via `just remote <host> <recipe>`.

---

## §4b — Surface assessment & climbs

Nine maintenance surfaces × rung (this repo is unusually high on the ladder already):

| # | Surface | Has it? | Rung | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **build** | yes | **test** | `nix flake check` evaluates every host config; nix's build graph *is* incremental + content-addressed. |
| 2 | **test** | yes (3 layers) | **test** | eval (layer-1, sub-sec) · nixosTest (layer-2, VM) · runtime-introspection (layer-3, `just test-*` against live fleet). No flaky-skip census needed — layers are deterministic; layer-3 is the only one that needs a deployed fleet. |
| 3 | **docs** | yes | **GENERATE** (6 reference docs, `docs-fresh`-gated) + **test** (`routing-coherence` for existence/routing) + survey (the WHY-pair reference docs) | Among the strongest doc postures seen — Reference is *generated and drift-gated*, exactly the skill's prescription. |
| 4 | **deps** (flake inputs) | partial | **survey/partial-test** | `flake.lock` is eval-pinned (a stale lock that won't eval fails the gate), but there's **no `--frozen`-style lock↔manifest drift *error*** and no unused-input report. `just update-flake` is the only mutation path. **CLIMB ↓** |
| 5 | **cruft** (dead code) | yes | **test** | `deadnix` (unused bindings) on the gate. No orphan-*file* sweep (Knip-equivalent), but nix's import graph makes orphan modules eval-dead. |
| 6 | **debt** (SATD) | informal | **survey** | No `TODO`/`FIXME` census check. `docs/invariants.md` "promotion work-list" *is* a tracked-health register (better than a debt census). **CLIMB ↓ (low priority)** |
| 7 | **security — secrets** | sops yes, **scan NO** | **structural** (encryption) but **NO secret-scan** | sops encryption is structural; `.env` gitignored. But there is **no `gitleaks`/trufflehog pre-commit or CI** despite a hard "don't commit secrets" rule. A plaintext secret slipped into a non-sops path would not be caught. **CLIMB — highest leverage ↓** |
| 8 | **architecture** (fitness functions) | yes, extensively | **test** | `every-service-has-fs-hardening`, `every-service-has-backup-intent`, `infra-concerns-have-tests`, `audience↔auth` assertion, `routing-coherence` — prose invariants already promoted to gate-breaking checks. Exemplary. The `nori.lint` TOML registry makes adding a grep-rule one block. |
| 9 | **decisions** (ADRs) | yes | **survey + lifecycle** | Append-only convention documented (README); statuses present (all 5 Accepted, none yet superseded). **No supersede-link lint** (orphan-link check). **CLIMB ↓ (low priority)** |

### Climbs (recommendations, ranked)

1. **secret-scan → test/gate (HIGHEST).** Add `gitleaks` as a pre-commit hook + a `checks.gitleaks` derivation (or CI step). A leaked secret is permanent (removal cosmetic, rotation mandatory); this is the cheapest, highest-consequence missing gate. Fits the existing `lint/checks/` + flake-check pattern exactly. *Caveat:* must allowlist `secrets/secrets.yaml` and `secrets/apps.yaml` (sops ciphertext) and public-cert/known-hosts entries — the repo already tracks which paths are legitimately secret-shaped, so the allowlist is short.
2. **deps lock-drift → test.** A `nix flake check`-time assertion that `flake.lock` is consistent with `flake.nix` inputs (no manifest input lacking a lock node), so a hand-edited input that wasn't re-locked *errors* rather than silently evaluating against a stale pin. Lower value than secret-scan (nix's eval already catches most lock incoherence), but closes the "cached-green on stale lock" class.
3. **ADR supersede-link lint → test (low).** A check that every `Status: Superseded by ADR-XXXX` points at an existing ADR and that the target back-links. Cheap; near-nil current catch rate (nothing superseded yet) — add when the first supersede lands.
4. **SATD census → survey+ (low).** A `TODO`/`FIXME`/`HACK` census diff flagging bare/orphaned markers. The invariants promotion work-list already covers the health-over-debt instinct, so this is marginal.

**Surfaces already at the ceiling (no climb):** build, test, docs-reference, cruft, architecture. This repo is the *target state* the skill's ladder describes for those five.

---

## §5 — Worked example

**Real change:** commit `b730691 feat(docs): generated reference for nori.{backups,fs,replicas} (audit #7)`
+ `32dd55c docs(claude): route to backups/fs/replicas generated schemas`.

A checkpoint over `git diff 3022085..32dd55c`:
- **Classify:** work-type `feat(docs)` / surface = three new `nori.<X>` schemas getting generated reference docs.
- **Gate (G1):** `nix flake check` — the **new `docs-fresh` cases** (`docs-backups`, `docs-fs`, `docs-replicas`) must pass: committed `docs/generated/<X>.md` byte-equals `nix build .#docs-<X>`. If the schema and committed doc disagree, gate fails with the diff inline; **autofix** = run the printed `nix build … && cp …` regen.
- **Survey (G2):** work-type `feat(<infra-concern>)` row → confirm-or-update: the WHY-pair reference docs (`docs/reference/storage.md` for fs/replicas; `docs/reference/services.md § backup` for backups), `CLAUDE.md` + `docs/README.md` routing tables now point at the new generated docs (`routing-coherence` gates this — it caught nothing because `32dd55c` added the routes), `docs/invariants.md` unchanged (no new *claim*, just new generated artifacts).
- **Handoff:** the commit message names the audit finding (#7) the work closes; the generated docs are evidence-bound (regenerable from source), separating done (3 schemas documented + routed) from any deferred follow-up.

This is the skill's loop running end-to-end: the gate's `docs-fresh` check *is* the GENERATE rung making doc-drift unrepresentable; `routing-coherence` is the TEST rung on doc existence; only the WHY-pair prose needed a hand survey.
